description: Main window. This file governs the Tauri *plugin* surface only, and grants the narrowest form of each: an ask/confirm dialog, restart for the updater, and two exact URLs for the opener. The WebView is granted NO filesystem permission: exporting a file goes through the Rust `export_save` command, which runs the save dialog and the write itself, so the WebView never supplies a path (security review TAURI-01 — it previously held an unscoped fs:allow-write-text-file). core:window:allow-destroy is listed because core:default grants only the read-only window commands, and the app's close guard ends in getCurrentWindow().destroy() — without it the ACL rejected that call, the promise rejection went nowhere, and the red X did nothing at all. TupleNest's own pg_* commands are Rust #[tauri::command]s registered in main.rs and are not ACL-gated here — nor are the two files the driver reads directly (a TLS CA and an SSH private key), which the user names in the connection form and which are read Rust-side, outside this scope. Said plainly because the previous wording ('every TupleNest command is explicitly listed; nothing broad is exposed') was not true of either.
